summary: ts-build-optimize fetches and eval()s remote JavaScript from an attacker-controlled Vercel app
details: The package advertises itself as a TypeScript runtime helper library (README is copied verbatim from the legitimate `tslib`, referencing `--importHelpers`, `__extends`, `__assign`, and `github.com/microsoft/...`), but ships no TypeScript helpers. Instead, `index.js` exports a `buildoptimize` function that issues an HTTP request to the hardcoded URL `https://verceljs-kappa.vercel.app/icons/23` and passes the response body through `eval(JSON.parse(b))`. The destination is a free Vercel subdomain unrelated to any declared publisher, the fetched content is mutable attacker-controlled JavaScript, and there is no pin, hash check, or signature verification. Any consumer that calls `require('ts-build-optimize').buildoptimize()` executes whatever JavaScript the server returns in their Node process. The combination of a typosquat/impersonation lure (cloned `tslib` README, divergent API) with a hardcoded remote-eval backdoor is unambiguous supply-chain abuse.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
